data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-04-29T01:39:37.481Z</current_datetime>

I'm migrating a .NET 10 web API to .NET 11. The app uses EF Core with the Azure Cosmos DB provider.
We have a repository pattern where some methods use synchronous LINQ:

```csharp
public class ProductRepository
{
    private readonly CosmosDbContext _context;

    public List<Product> GetAll()
    {
        return _context.Products.ToList();
    }

    public Product GetById(int id)
    {
        return _context.Products.First(p => p.Id == id);
    }

    public int GetCount()
    {
        return _context.Products.Count();
    }

    public void Save(Product product)
    {
        _context.Products.Add(product);
        _context.SaveChanges();
    }
}
```

In .NET 10 we had the sync-over-async opt-in configured. What breaks in .NET 11?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about what breaks when migrating from .NET 10 to .NET 11 with EF Core using the Azure Cosmos DB provider, specifically regarding synchronous LINQ operations.

This is a knowledge question about .NET/EF Core breaking changes. Let me provide what I know:

In EF Core's Cosmos DB provider, there has been a progression of changes around synchronous API support:

1. The Cosmos DB provider has historically only supported async operations natively (since the underlying Azure Cosmos DB SDK is async-only).

2. In EF Core 10 (.NET 10), synchronous operations on the Cosmos provider were deprecated but could still work with an opt-in configuration. The opt-in was something like `CosmosOptionsExtension` with `EnableSyncExecution` or similar.


3. The Cosmos provider's synchronous operations were never truly native—they've always been implemented as sync-over-async wrappers, which is why the team has been moving toward requiring explicit async-only usage.

In the .NET 10 to .NET 11 transition, the `EnableSyncExecution()` opt-in that allowed these wrappers gets removed entirely, so any synchronous LINQ operations like `ToList()`, `First()`, `Count()`, or `SaveChanges()` will throw `InvalidOperationException`. The migration path is straightforward: convert all sync calls to their async counterparts like `ToListAsync()` and `FirstAsync()`.

I should note that since we're in April 2026, .NET 11 is likely still in preview at this point, with the official release expected in November, so the user is probably working against a preview build of this breaking change.

The core issue is that .NET 11 and EF Core 11 remove the `EnableSyncExecution()` workaround for the Cosmos DB provider entirely, meaning any synchronous LINQ operations like `ToList()`, `First()`, `Count()`, or `Single()` will throw an `InvalidOperationException`. The fix requires converting all these sync calls to their async counterparts and making the calling methods async as well.
